title: Carphalea involucrata
taxonomy: Carphalea involucrata is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It belongs to the tribe Spermacoceae, which includes several other plant genera. The plant is native to Madagascar and is part of the genus Carphalea. Carphalea involucrata is a flowering plant that is characterized by its small white flowers and hairy leaves. Its classification is further supported by molecular phylogenetic studies within the Rubiaceae family.
uses: Carphalea involucrata has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits. Its leaves are known to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help in reducing pain and inflammation. The plant is also used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues. Additionally, Carphalea involucrata is believed to have antimalarial properties, making it a potential treatment for malaria. Further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety as a medicinal plant.

The plant's leaves and stems contain compounds with antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and fight off infections. preparations: Carphalea involucrata, a plant native to tropical Africa, has been traditionally used in herbal preparations for its medicinal properties. intro: Carphalea involucrata, also known as the Hawaiian hawthorn or ʻōlena, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including the reduction of inflammation, improvement of cardiovascular health, and the potential to lower cholesterol levels. The bioactive constituents of Carphalea involucrata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Hawaiian medicine, Carphalea involucrata is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to promote overall well-being and to support cardiovascular health.
